Interface ReactiveListOperations<K,V>

public interface ReactiveListOperations<K,V>
Reactive Redis operations for List Commands.

Streams of methods returning Mono<K> or Flux<M> are terminated with InvalidDataAccessApiUsageException when RedisElementReader.read(ByteBuffer) returns null for a particular element as Reactive Streams prohibit the usage of null values.

Since:
2.0
Author:
Mark Paluch, Christoph Strobl, John Blum
See Also:
  • Redis Documentation: List Commands

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Mono<Boolean>
    delete(K key)
    Removes the given key.
    default @Nullable Mono<V>
    getFirst(K key)
    Returns the first element from the list at key.
    default @Nullable Mono<V>
    getLast(K key)
    Returns the last element from the list at key.
    Mono<V>
    index(K key, long index)
    Get element at index from list at key.
    Mono<Long>
    indexOf(K key, V value)
    Returns the index of the first occurrence of the specified value in the list at at key.
    Mono<Long>
    lastIndexOf(K key, V value)
    Returns the index of the last occurrence of the specified value in the list at at key.
    Mono<V>
    leftPop(K key)
    Removes and returns first element in list stored at key.
    Flux<V>
    leftPop(K key, long count)
    Removes count elements from the left-side of the Redis list stored at key.
    Mono<V>
    leftPop(K key, Duration timeout)
    Removes and returns first element from lists stored at key.
    Mono<Long>
    leftPush(K key, V value)
    Prepend value to key.
    Mono<Long>
    leftPush(K key, V pivot, V value)
    Insert value to key before pivot.
    Mono<Long>
    leftPushAll(K key, Collection<V> values)
    Prepend values to key.
    Mono<Long>
    leftPushAll(K key, V... values)
    Prepend values to key.
    Mono<Long>
    leftPushIfPresent(K key, V value)
    Prepend values to key only if the list exists.
    Mono<V>
    move(K sourceKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction from, K destinationKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction to)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    Mono<V>
    move(K sourceKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction from, K destinationKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction to, Duration timeout)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    default Mono<V>
    move(ListOperations.MoveFrom<K> from, ListOperations.MoveTo<K> to)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    default Mono<V>
    move(ListOperations.MoveFrom<K> from, ListOperations.MoveTo<K> to, Duration timeout)
    Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
    Flux<V>
    range(K key, long start, long end)
    Get elements between begin and end from list at key.
    Mono<Long>
    remove(K key, long count, Object value)
    Removes the first count occurrences of value from the list stored at key.
    Mono<V>
    rightPop(K key)
    Removes and returns last element in list stored at key.
    Flux<V>
    rightPop(K key, long count)
    Removes count elements from the right-side of the Redis list stored at key.
    Mono<V>
    rightPop(K key, Duration timeout)
    Removes and returns last element from lists stored at key.
    Mono<V>
    rightPopAndLeftPush(K sourceKey, K destinationKey)
    Remove the last element from list at sourceKey, append it to destinationKey and return its value.
    Mono<V>
    rightPopAndLeftPush(K sourceKey, K destinationKey, Duration timeout)
    Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
    Results return once an element available or timeout reached.
    Mono<Long>
    rightPush(K key, V value)
    Append value to key.
    Mono<Long>
    rightPush(K key, V pivot, V value)
    Insert value to key after pivot.
    Mono<Long>
    rightPushAll(K key, Collection<V> values)
    Append values to key.
    Mono<Long>
    rightPushAll(K key, V... values)
    Append values to key.
    Mono<Long>
    rightPushIfPresent(K key, V value)
    Append values to key only if the list exists.
    Mono<Boolean>
    set(K key, long index, V value)
    Set the value list element at index.
    Mono<Long>
    size(K key)
    Get the size of list stored at key.
    Mono<Boolean>
    trim(K key, long start, long end)
    Trim list at key to elements between start and end.

    • range

      Flux<V> range(K key, long start, long end)
      Get elements between begin and end from list at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      start -
      end -
      Returns:

    • move

      default Mono<V> move(ListOperations.MoveFrom<K> from, ListOperations.MoveTo<K> to)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
      Parameters:
      from - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LMOVE

    • move

      Mono<V> move(K sourceKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction from, K destinationKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction to)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.
      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      from - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LMOVE

    • move

      default Mono<V> move(ListOperations.MoveFrom<K> from, ListOperations.MoveTo<K> to, Duration timeout)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.

      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.

      Parameters:
      from - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      timeout -
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BLMOVE

    • move

      Mono<V> move(K sourceKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction from, K destinationKey, ReactiveListCommands.Direction to, Duration timeout)
      Atomically returns and removes the first/last element (head/tail depending on the from argument) of the list stored at sourceKey, and pushes the element at the first/last element (head/tail depending on the to argument) of the list stored at destinationKey.

      Blocks connection until element available or timeout reached.

      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      from - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      to - must not be null.
      timeout -
      Returns:
      null when used in pipeline / transaction.
      Since:
      2.6
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BLMOVE

      Mono<Boolean> set(K key, long index, V value)

    • indexOf

      Mono<Long> indexOf(K key, V value)
      Returns the index of the first occurrence of the specified value in the list at at key.
      Requires Redis 6.0.6 or newer.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.4
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOS

    • lastIndexOf

      Mono<Long> lastIndexOf(K key, V value)
      Returns the index of the last occurrence of the specified value in the list at at key.
      Requires Redis 6.0.6 or newer.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      value - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Since:
      2.4
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOS

    • leftPop

      Mono<V> leftPop(K key)
      Removes and returns first element in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOP

    • leftPop

      Flux<V> leftPop(K key, long count)
      Removes count elements from the left-side of the Redis list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count - count of the number of elements to remove from the left-side of the Redis list.
      Returns:
      a Flux containing the elements removed from the Redis list.
      Since:
      3.2
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: LPOP

    • leftPop

      Mono<V> leftPop(K key, Duration timeout)
      Removes and returns first element from lists stored at key.
      Results return once an element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - maximal duration to wait until an entry in the list at key is available. Must be either Duration.ZERO or greater 1 second, must not be null. A timeout of zero can be used to wait indefinitely. Durations between zero and one second are not supported. Durations are expected to be in whole seconds so only the seconds field is used - the other fields (e.g. nanos) are ignored.
      Returns:
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if timeout is between 0 and 1 sec
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BLPOP

    • rightPop

      Mono<V> rightPop(K key)
      Removes and returns last element in list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOP

    • rightPop

      Flux<V> rightPop(K key, long count)
      Removes count elements from the right-side of the Redis list stored at key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      count - count of the number of elements to remove from the right-side of the Redis list.
      Returns:
      a Flux containing the elements removed from the Redis list.
      Since:
      3.2
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOP

    • rightPop

      Mono<V> rightPop(K key, Duration timeout)
      Removes and returns last element from lists stored at key.
      Results return once an element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
      timeout - maximal duration to wait until an entry in the list at key is available. Must be either Duration.ZERO or greater 1 second, must not be null. A timeout of zero can be used to wait indefinitely. Durations between zero and one second are not supported. Durations are expected to be in whole seconds so only the seconds field is used - the other fields (e.g. nanos) are ignored.
      Returns:
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if timeout is between 0 and 1 sec
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BRPOP

    • rightPopAndLeftPush

      Mono<V> rightPopAndLeftPush(K sourceKey, K destinationKey)
      Remove the last element from list at sourceKey, append it to destinationKey and return its value.
      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: RPOPLPUSH

    • rightPopAndLeftPush

      Mono<V> rightPopAndLeftPush(K sourceKey, K destinationKey, Duration timeout)
      Remove the last element from list at srcKey, append it to dstKey and return its value.
      Results return once an element available or timeout reached.
      Parameters:
      sourceKey - must not be null.
      destinationKey - must not be null.
      timeout - maximal duration to wait until an entry in the list at sourceKey is available. Must be either Duration.ZERO or greater 1 second, must not be null. A timeout of zero can be used to wait indefinitely. Durations between zero and one second are not supported. Durations are expected to be in whole seconds so only the seconds field is used - the other fields (e.g. nanos) are ignored.
      Returns:
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if timeout is between 0 and 1 sec
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: BRPOPLPUSH

    • delete

      Mono<Boolean> delete(K key)
      Removes the given key.
      Parameters:
      key - must not be null.
